summary: Tight second set with standout China>Rider>Eyes and a soaring Wharf Rat
blurb: The show shines in the second set where China Cat Sunflower segues into I Know You Rider and flows into an expansive Eyes Of The World. Jerry delivers solid lead work, especially on the Eyes finale, while Phil’s bass drives the band forward. Highlights also include a slow‑burning Wharf Rat that builds tension before the drums.
review: December 7, 1979 at the Indiana Convention Center captured a solid ’79 Dead show that still feels fresh for listeners today. The second set is the real prize: a superb China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition that rolls straight into an inspired Eyes Of The World, with Jerry’s guitar soaring on the finale and Phil front‑and‑center in the mix, thumping out bombs that propel the band. Brent Mydland clicks musically, adding depth without ever stepping on the groove, while the rhythm section stays tight beneath a slow‑burning Wharf Rat that builds tension before the drums take over. The first set offers reliable workmanlike performances of classics like Althea and The Music Never Stopped, but it’s the second‑set flow and the occasional jam‑before‑drums moment that make this concert a worthwhile listen. Despite some recordings suffering from phase issues, the best soundboard capture presents a clear, crisp mix that showcases the band’s chemistry on a night when everything clicked together.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is clearly positive, praising the China>Rider>Eyes sequence and strong band energy; two recordings are mixed but still note solid performance (Wharf Rat highlight, overall good flow); one negative focuses solely on out‑of‑phase audio, not musical execution. The consensus leans positively toward the show’s playing, justifying a moderate +1.0 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard mix that highlights Phil’s bass and captures the superb second‑set flow, especially the China>Rider>Eyes sequence.

key_highlights: Superb China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider → Eyes Of The World segues
Intense, slow‑building Wharf Rat before the drums
Phil Lesh’s bass front‑and‑center, driving the band’s energy
Brent Mydland’s tasteful keyboard work adds depth

band_performance.Jerry: Fine night on guitar, especially loving the Eyes of the World finale.
band_performance.Phil: Out front in the mix, thumping, throwing bombs left and right; a key driver of the show’s energy.
band_performance.Brent: Clicks musically with the band, adding depth to the performance.
